cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the text suggests, and the options are A to D. I need to figure out which one is correct based on the given passage. First, let me recall the content of the passage. The main subject is Zhao Wenxuan, a Taiwanese film star. The text talks about how he became interested in films as a child, worked in an airline before acting, and then got into acting by chance when he responded to an ad for the film "Wedding Feast." That film was directed by Li An and apparently was a big success. It won awards like the German Golden Bear and was in the Oscar competition. It was the first Taiwan film allowed in the U.S. Because of this film, Zhao became popular in Taiwan, Hong Kong, and Southeast Asia. Then later he did other films like "Red Rose and White Rose" and has been active since 1993. He also mentions cooperation between Taiwan and the mainland in film production. Now, the options are: A) "Wedding Feast" is just an ordinary film B) The film "Wedding Feast" disappointed people greatly C) Zhao Wenxuan became a famous movie star because of the film "Wedding Feast" D) A lot of the Taiwan films are shown in the U.S. every year Looking at the passage, the text says that "Wedding Feast" was a hit, won awards, and was the first Taiwan film permitted in the U.S. Also, it's stated that thanks to this film, Zhao became popular. So option C seems correct because the text directly links his fame to this film. Option A is the opposite of what's stated since the film was successful. Option B is also incorrect as it's the opposite of the text's claims. Option D is tricky but the text only
